message:
test(ft-conformance): linalg.qr numpy subprocess conformance [bd-c36b]

Adds torch_linalg_qr_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering 7
matrices: square 3x3, 4x4 identity, tall 4x2 / 5x3, square upper
triangular 3x3 (Q ≈ I), 1x1 scalar, and a 2x2 with negative
determinant.

Element-wise comparison of Q and R is fragile because QR is unique
only up to column sign — different LAPACK builds flip signs of Q's
columns and R's rows. The harness instead verifies the two
structural invariants that uniquely characterize a valid QR:
  - reconstruction:  Q @ R ≈ A  (matches numpy element-wise)
  - orthogonality:   Q^T @ Q ≈ I_k

Both compared via per-element relative tolerance 1e-9. This is the
standard QR conformance pattern that's robust to sign flips while
still catching real bugs (wrong factorization, broken
orthogonalization, off-by-one in the reduced/full mode plumbing).

Sister harness to the inv / solve / cholesky / det / slogdet
harnesses. Files closure for the qr slice of frankentorch-c36b.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
